title: Here's What We're Asking Oklahoma Candidates
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States explains the eight-statement questionnaire it sent to Oklahoma State House and Senate candidates ahead of 2020 endorsements, designed to determine whether candidates will fully support immediate abolition of abortion and reject incremental pro-life bills. The piece frames the questionnaire as a litmus test for replacing outgoing Senator Joseph Silk with similarly bold abolitionist statesmen.
audiences: Candidate considering filling out the questionnaire
Pro-life activist evaluating the abolitionist position
Pastor or church leader discipling political engagement
Out-of-state abolitionist organizer designing a similar tool

questions.question: How many Oklahoma legislative candidates were sent the questionnaire and how many were unreachable?
questions.answer: Free the States reached 227 of 261 candidates running for State House and Senate; the remaining 34 had no campaign website or Facebook page as of the prior Saturday and would be re-attempted the following week.
questions.quote: 227 of the 261 have been sent the questionnaire with 34 candidates not having a campaign website or Facebook page as of last Saturday.

questions.question: What format does the questionnaire use and how can candidates elaborate on their answers?
questions.audience: Candidate considering filling out the questionnaire
questions.answer: It is a set of eight statements, each answered with a Yes or No checkbox indicating agreement or disagreement, with an optional text box beneath each statement for further explanation.
questions.quote: After each statement, the candidate can check either ‘Yes’ or “No’ to indicate their agreement or disagreement with the statement. There is also an optional text box under each statement for the candidate to explain their answer further if they so desire.

questions.question: What is the very first commitment Free the States asks candidates to make?
questions.audience: Candidate considering filling out the questionnaire
questions.answer: Statement one asks the candidate to pledge to author or coauthor the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (or an equivalently named bill) and to vote in favor of it.
questions.quote: I will author or coauthor the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act – or an equivalent act by a different name – and will vote in favor of it.

questions.question: Does the questionnaire ask candidates to defy their own party leadership?
questions.audience: Pro-life activist evaluating the abolitionist position
questions.answer: Yes — statement three explicitly asks candidates to commit to abolition even when it requires standing up to the Republican and pro-life establishments.
questions.quote: I will do these things in support of abolishing abortion even if it means standing up to the Republican and pro-life establishments.

questions.question: On what theological and legal grounds does the questionnaire reject incremental pro-life bills?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader discipling political engagement
questions.answer: Statement six holds that incremental pro-life bills regulate and therefore allow the murder of human beings, which is characterized as both unBiblical and unconstitutional.
questions.quote: I agree that incremental pro-life bills, by regulating and therefore allowing the murder of human beings, are both unBiblical and unconstitutional.

questions.question: What is the overall purpose of the questionnaire and the kind of candidate Free the States hopes to elect?
questions.audience: Out-of-state abolitionist organizer designing a similar tool
questions.answer: It is built to identify candidates willing to do everything in their power for total and immediate abolition, to stand up to the establishment, and to oppose compromise — replacements in the mold of outgoing Senator Joseph Silk.
questions.quote: Our questionnaire was designed to discover whether the candidate intends to do everything in his or her power to support abortion’s total and immediate abolition, whether the candidate is prepared to stand up to the establishment, and whether the candidate will oppose compromise.

questions.question: What did the early returns from candidates look like?
questions.answer: Most candidates expected to agree with all eight statements did so, and a few candidates who had not previously been on Free the States' radar returned perfect questionnaires.
questions.quote: Most of the candidates we expected to agree with all eight statements have done so. Even more good news: A few candidates who weren’t even on our radar a few weeks ago sent us perfect questionnaires!

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Statement seven of the questionnaire explicitly cites this article as the explanation for why incremental pro-life bills dehumanize preborn children.

related.title: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
related.reason: The questionnaire is framed as a search for replacements for departing Senator Joseph Silk, the abolitionist statesman the article holds up as the standard.

related.title: "I am a pro-life Oklahoman who voted to table a bill that would abolish all abortion"
related.reason: Illustrates the establishment behavior that statements three and eight ask candidates to repudiate — a pro-life legislator killing an abolition bill.

related.title: Oklahomans for Life Asks Candidates to Oppose Abortion's Immediate Abolition
related.reason: Published the same month as a direct counterpoint: a rival questionnaire from the pro-life establishment Free the States is asking candidates to defy.

related.title: National Right to Life Endorses Greg McCortney, the Senator Who Kept Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Concrete example of the pro-life establishment endorsements the questionnaire is meant to push abolitionist candidates to oppose.

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Follow-up coverage of the 2020 primary cycle the questionnaire fed into, showing how an abolitionist candidate fared against the establishment.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Provides the broader Oklahoma church-and-politics context for why a candidate questionnaire centered on immediate abolition was timely in 2020.
